revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]にある[[神社]]。[[城崎温泉]]の[[外湯]]の一つ「御所の湯」の隣に...'
revision.text:
'''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]にある[[神社]]。[[城崎温泉]]の[[外湯]]の一つ「御所の湯」の隣に鎮座する。 [[画像:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|thumb|300px|神門、拝殿]]
==概要== [[708年]]（[[和銅]]元年）に日生下権守が夢に現れた4人の神より託れて創建した。[[717年]]（[[養老]]元年）には当社に参拝した道智に神託があり、外湯「まんだら湯」を開湯したと伝わる。以来、城崎温泉の守護神として奉られてきた。 四柱の祭神は温泉守護神で主祭神の湯山主神と水の守護神である宗像三女神である。
本堂裏から名水「延命水」が湧出する。
==祭り== *[[城崎だんじり祭り]]（例祭） - 10月14日（宵宮）～10月15日（本宮） *城崎七夕まつり
==文化財== *本殿 - 三間社流れ造り、[[1925年]]（大正14年）[[北但馬地震]]で全壊後の再建。兵庫県登録有形文化財 *拝殿 - 入母屋造、平屋建、1925年（大正14年）北但馬地震で全壊後の再建。兵庫県登録有形文化財
==所在地・アクセス== *所在地 - 〒669-6101 兵庫県城崎郡城崎町湯島447 *交通アクセス - [[山陰本線]] [[城崎温泉駅]] 徒歩15分
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}}
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししよしんしや}}
[[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市]]

'''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]にある[[神社]]。[[城崎温泉]]の[[外湯]]の一つ「御所の湯」の隣に鎮座する。 [[画像:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|thumb|300px|神門、拝殿]]
==概要== [[708年]]（[[和銅]]元年）に日生下権守が夢に現れた4人の神より託れて創建した。[[717年]]（[[養老]]元年）には当社に参拝した道智に神託があり、外湯「まんだら湯」を開湯したと伝わる。以来、城崎温泉の守護神として奉られてきた。 四柱の祭神は温泉守護神で主祭神の湯山主神と水の守護神である宗像三女神である。
本堂裏から名水「延命水」が湧出する。
==祭事== *[[城崎だんじり祭り]]（例祭） - 10月14日（宵宮）～10月15日（本宮） *[[城崎七夕まつり]]
==文化財== *本殿 - 三間社流れ造り、[[1925年]]（大正14年）[[北但馬地震]]で全壊後の再建。兵庫県登録有形文化財 *拝殿 - 入母屋造、平屋建、1925年（大正14年）北但馬地震で全壊後の再建。兵庫県登録有形文化財
==所在地・アクセス== *所在地 - 〒669-6101 兵庫県城崎郡城崎町湯島447 *交通アクセス - [[山陰本線]] [[城崎温泉駅]] 徒歩15分
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}}

'''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]にある[[神社]]。[[城崎温泉]]の[[外湯]]の一つ「御所の湯」の隣に鎮座する。 [[画像:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|thumb|300px|神門、拝殿]]

==祭事== *[[城崎だんじり祭り]]（例祭） - 10月14日（宵宮）～10月15日（本宮） *[[城崎七夕まつり]]
==文化財== *豊岡市指定文化財建造物 **弁天山宝篋印塔「越中次郎兵衛の墓」1基 - [[1369年]]（[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]・[[応安]]2年）建立（昭和50年3月1日指定） *豊岡市指定文化財天然記念物 **[[ヒメハルゼミ]]の発生の地（平成2年5月18日指定） *兵庫県登録有形文化財建造物 **本殿 - 三間社流れ造り、[[1925年]]（[[大正]]14年）[[北但馬地震]]で全壊後の[[1928年]]（[[昭和]]3年）の再建（平成18年12月22日登録） **拝殿 - 入母屋造、平屋建、1925年（大正14年）北但馬地震で全壊後の1928年（昭和3年）の再建（平成18年12月22日登録）
==所在地・アクセス== *所在地 - 〒669-6101 兵庫県城崎郡城崎町湯島447 *交通アクセス - [[山陰本線]] [[城崎温泉駅]] 徒歩15分

==文化財== *豊岡市指定文化財建造物 **弁天山宝篋印塔「越中次郎兵衛の墓」1基 - [[1369年]]（[[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]・[[応安]]2年）建立（昭和50年3月1日指定） *豊岡市指定文化財天然記念物 **[[ヒメハルゼミ]]の発生の地（平成2年5月18日指定） *兵庫県登録有形文化財建造物 **本殿 - 三間社流れ造り、[[1925年]]（[[大正]]14年）[[北但馬地震]]で全壊後の[[1928年]]（[[昭和]]3年）の再建（平成18年12月22日登録） **拝殿 - 入母屋造、平屋建、1925年（大正14年）北但馬地震で全壊後の1928年（昭和3年）の再建（平成18年12月22日登録）

{{神社 |名称 = 四所神社 |画像 = [[画像:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
神門、拝殿 |所在地 = 兵庫県豊岡市城崎町湯島447 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 湯山主神 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝和銅元年（708年） |本殿 = 三間社流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月15日]] |神事 = 城崎だんじり祭り（10月14日-15日）
城崎七夕まつり（7月5日-7日） }} '''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]にある[[神社]]。[[城崎温泉]]の[[外湯]]の一つ「御所の湯」の隣に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == '''湯山主神'''（ゆやまぬしのかみ）を主祭神に、[[多紀理毘売命|多岐里比売神（たきりひめのかみ）]]、[[多岐都比売命|田記津比売神（たきつひめのかみ）]]、[[市杵島比売命|市杵比売神（いちきひめのかみ）]]の[[宗像三女神]]を配祀する。いずれも[[温泉]]の[[守護神]]として崇められ、また宗像三女神は水の守護神でもある。 == 由緒 == [[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]）に日生下権守という者が夢中に現れた4柱の神の[[神託]]に従って創建した。[[養老]]元年（[[717年]]）には当社に参拝した[[道智]]に神託があり、外湯「まんだら湯」を開湯したと伝わる。以来、城崎温泉の守護神として、道智開基の[[温泉寺 (豊岡市)|温泉寺]]を[[別当寺]]として崇敬を集めたが、[[明治]]の[[神仏判然令]]で明治3年（[[1870年]]）に同寺と分離し、同6年に[[村社]]に列した。 本殿裏から名水「延命水」が湧出する。 == 祭事 == * [[城崎だんじり祭り]]（例祭） - 10月14日（宵宮）～10月15日（本宮） * [[城崎七夕まつり]] - 7月5日～7月7日 == 社殿 == 現在の社殿は、[[大正]]14年（[[1925年]]）の[[北但馬地震]]で古記録と共に全壊、焼失したため、[[昭和]]3年（[[1928年]]）に再建されたもの。[[本殿]]は[[流造|三間社流造]]、[[拝殿]]は[[入母屋造]]平屋建で共に県の有形文化財。昭和56年（[[1981年]]）に[[和田政春]]の設計、施工による改修が行われている。因みに旧本殿は[[寛延]]4年（[[1751年]]）の建立にかかる総欅造りの広壮なものであった。 == 文化財 == === 兵庫県指定 === 登録有形文化財建造物 * 本殿（[[平成]]18年（[[2006年]]）12月22日登録） * 拝殿（同上） === 豊岡市指定 === 文化財建造物 * 弁天山宝篋印塔「越中次郎兵衛の墓」1基 - [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]の[[応安]]2年（[[1369年]]）の建立（昭和50年3月1日指定） 天然記念物 * [[ヒメハルゼミ]]の発生の地（平成2年5月18日指定） == 交通 == * [[山陰本線]] [[城崎温泉駅]] 徒歩15分 == 関連項目 == * [[四所神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html 四所神社]（兵庫県神社庁） * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/infomation/rekisi/jisya/jisya.html#rekisi02 四所神社]・[http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/infomation/maturi/danjiri.html だんじり祭り]（城崎温泉観光協会） {{Kinosaki onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししよしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:温泉神社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:豊岡市]]

{{神社 |名称 = 四所神社 |画像 = [[画像:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
神門、拝殿 |所在地 = 兵庫県豊岡市城崎町湯島447 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000}} |祭神 = 湯山主神 |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝和銅元年（708年） |本殿 = 三間社流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月15日]] |神事 = 城崎だんじり祭り（10月14日-15日）
城崎七夕まつり（7月5日-7日） }} '''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]にある[[神社]]。[[城崎温泉]]の[[外湯]]の一つ「御所の湯」の隣に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == '''湯山主神'''（ゆやまぬしのかみ）を主祭神に、[[多紀理毘売命|多岐里比売神（たきりひめのかみ）]]、[[多岐都比売命|田記津比売神（たきつひめのかみ）]]、[[市杵島比売命|市杵比売神（いちきひめのかみ）]]の[[宗像三女神]]を配祀する。いずれも[[温泉]]の[[守護神]]として崇められ、また宗像三女神は水の守護神でもある。 == 由緒 == [[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]）に日生下権守という者が夢中に現れた4柱の神の[[神託]]に従って創建した。[[養老]]元年（[[717年]]）には当社に参拝した[[道智]]に神託があり、外湯「まんだら湯」を開湯したと伝わる。以来、城崎温泉の守護神として、道智開基の[[温泉寺 (豊岡市)|温泉寺]]を[[別当寺]]として崇敬を集めたが、[[明治]]の[[神仏判然令]]で明治3年（[[1870年]]）に同寺と分離し、同6年に[[村社]]に列した。 本殿裏から名水「延命水」が湧出する。 == 祭事 == * [[城崎だんじり祭り]]（例祭） - 10月14日（宵宮）～10月15日（本宮） * [[城崎七夕まつり]] - 7月5日～7月7日 == 社殿 == 現在の社殿は、[[大正]]14年（[[1925年]]）の[[北但馬地震]]で古記録と共に全壊、焼失したため、[[昭和]]3年（[[1928年]]）に再建されたもの。[[本殿]]は[[流造|三間社流造]]、[[拝殿]]は[[入母屋造]]平屋建で共に県の有形文化財。昭和56年（[[1981年]]）に[[和田政春]]の設計、施工による改修が行われている。因みに旧本殿は[[寛延]]4年（[[1751年]]）の建立にかかる総欅造りの広壮なものであった。 == 文化財 == === 兵庫県指定 === 登録有形文化財建造物 * 本殿（[[平成]]18年（[[2006年]]）12月22日登録） * 拝殿（同上） === 豊岡市指定 === 文化財建造物 * 弁天山宝篋印塔「越中次郎兵衛の墓」1基 - [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]の[[応安]]2年（[[1369年]]）の建立（昭和50年3月1日指定） 天然記念物 * [[ヒメハルゼミ]]の発生の地（平成2年5月18日指定） == 交通 == * [[山陰本線]] [[城崎温泉駅]] 徒歩15分 == 関連項目 == * [[四所神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html 四所神社]（兵庫県神社庁） * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/infomation/rekisi/jisya/jisya.html#rekisi02 四所神社]・[http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/infomation/maturi/danjiri.html だんじり祭り]（城崎温泉観光協会） {{Kinosaki onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししよしんしや}} [[Category:豊岡市の神社]] [[Category:温泉神社]] [[Category:村社]]

温泉祭り(4月23日) }} '''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]にある[[神社]]。[[城崎温泉]]の[[外湯]]の一つ「御所の湯」の隣に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == '''湯山主神'''（ゆやまぬしのかみ）を主祭神に、[[多紀理毘売命|多岐里比売神（たきりひめのかみ）]]、[[多岐都比売命|田記津比売神（たきつひめのかみ）]]、[[市杵島比売命|市杵比売神（いちきひめのかみ）]]の[[宗像三女神]]を配し合わせて四所四神を祀る。いずれも[[温泉]]の[[守護神]]として崇められ、また[[宗像三女神]]は水の守護神でもある。 == 由緒 == [[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]）に日生下権守という者が夢中に現れた4柱の神の[[神託]]に従って創建した。[[養老]]元年（[[717年]]）には当社に参拝した[[道智]]に神託があり、外湯「まんだら湯」を開湯したと伝わる。以来、城崎温泉の守護神として、道智開基の[[温泉寺 (豊岡市)|温泉寺]]を[[別当寺]]として崇敬を集めたが、[[明治]]の[[神仏判然令]]で明治3年（[[1870年]]）に同寺と分離し、同6年に[[村社]]に列した。 本殿裏から名水「延命水」が湧出する。 == 祭事 == * [[城崎だんじり祭り]]（例祭） - 10月14日（宵宮）～10月15日（本宮） *[[城崎温泉祭り]] 4月23日 == 社殿 == 現在の社殿は、[[大正]]14年（[[1925年]]）の[[北但馬地震]]で古記録と共に全壊、焼失したため、[[昭和]]3年（[[1928年]]）に再建されたもの。[[本殿]]は[[流造|三間社流造]]、[[拝殿]]は[[入母屋造]]平屋建で共に県の有形文化財。昭和56年（[[1981年]]）に[[和田政春]]の設計、施工による改修が行われている。因みに旧本殿は[[寛延]]4年（[[1751年]]）の建立にかかる総欅造りの広壮なものであった。 == 文化財 == === 兵庫県指定 === 登録有形文化財建造物 * 本殿（[[平成]]18年（[[2006年]]）12月22日登録） * 拝殿（同上） === 豊岡市指定 === 文化財建造物 * 弁天山宝篋印塔「越中次郎兵衛の墓」1基 - [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]の[[応安]]2年（[[1369年]]）の建立（昭和50年3月1日指定） 天然記念物 * [[ヒメハルゼミ]]の発生の地（平成2年5月18日指定） == 交通 == * [[山陰本線]] [[城崎温泉駅]] 徒歩15分 == 関連項目 == * [[四所神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html 四所神社]（兵庫県神社庁） * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html 温泉寺、四所神社、極楽寺]（城崎温泉観光協会） {{Kinosaki onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししよしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:温泉神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]]

神門、拝殿 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]城崎町湯島447 |位置 = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[湯山主大神]] |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝[[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]） |本殿 = 三間社流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月15日]] |神事 = 城崎だんじり祭り（10月14日-15日）

{{神社 |名称 = 四所神社 |画像 = [[画像:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
神門、拝殿 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]城崎町湯島447 |位置 = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[湯山主大神]] |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝[[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]） |本殿 = 三間社流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月15日]] |神事 = 城崎だんじり祭り（10月14日-15日）
温泉祭り(4月23日) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=四所神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]にある[[神社]]。[[城崎温泉]]の[[外湯]]の一つ「御所の湯」の隣に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == '''湯山主神'''（ゆやまぬしのかみ）を主祭神に、[[多紀理毘売命|多岐里比売神（たきりひめのかみ）]]、[[多岐都比売命|田記津比売神（たきつひめのかみ）]]、[[市杵島比売命|市杵比売神（いちきひめのかみ）]]の[[宗像三女神]]を配し合わせて四所四神を祀る。いずれも[[温泉]]の[[守護神]]として崇められ、また[[宗像三女神]]は水の守護神でもある。 == 由緒 == [[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]）に日生下権守という者が夢中に現れた4柱の神の[[神託]]に従って創建した。[[養老]]元年（[[717年]]）には当社に参拝した[[道智]]に神託があり、外湯「まんだら湯」を開湯したと伝わる。以来、城崎温泉の守護神として、道智開基の[[温泉寺 (豊岡市)|温泉寺]]を[[別当寺]]として崇敬を集めたが、[[明治]]の[[神仏判然令]]で明治3年（[[1870年]]）に同寺と分離し、同6年に[[村社]]に列した。 本殿裏から名水「延命水」が湧出する。 == 祭事 == * [[城崎温泉祭り]] - [[4月23日]]～[[4月24日]]　城崎温泉の開祖である道智上人の開山忌を祈念して行われる。 *城崎温泉夢広場 - [[7月23日]]～[[8月25日]]（お盆・土日祝日を除く20：00～21：00）神社の境内で、スーパーボールすくいなどの屋台やパフォーマンスショーなどのイベントが行われる。また、同期間中、21時から5分間、約200発の夢花火が打ち上げられる。（[[8月10日]]～[[8月16日]]を除く。） *[[城崎だんじり祭り]]（例祭） - [[10月14日]]（宵宮）～[[10月15日]]（本宮） == 社殿 == 現在の社殿は、[[大正]]14年（[[1925年]]）の[[北但馬地震]]で古記録と共に全壊、焼失したため、[[昭和]]3年（[[1928年]]）に再建されたもの。[[本殿]]は[[流造|三間社流造]]、[[拝殿]]は[[入母屋造]]平屋建で共に県の有形文化財。昭和56年（[[1981年]]）に[[和田政春]]の設計、施工による改修が行われている。因みに旧本殿は[[寛延]]4年（[[1751年]]）の建立にかかる総欅造りの広壮なものであった。 == 文化財 == === 兵庫県指定 === 登録有形文化財建造物 * 本殿（[[平成]]18年（[[2006年]]）12月22日登録） * 拝殿（同上） === 豊岡市指定 === 文化財建造物 * 弁天山宝篋印塔「越中次郎兵衛の墓」1基 - [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]の[[応安]]2年（[[1369年]]）の建立（昭和50年3月1日指定） 天然記念物 * [[ヒメハルゼミ]]の発生の地（平成2年5月18日指定） == 交通 == * [[山陰本線]] [[城崎温泉駅]] 徒歩15分 == 関連項目 == * [[四所神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html 四所神社]（兵庫県神社庁） * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html 温泉寺、四所神社、極楽寺]（城崎温泉観光協会） {{神道 横}} {{Kinosaki onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししよしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:温泉神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Shisho Shrine |Image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |Location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|en|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|en|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|en|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|en|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|en|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takaririhime no Mikoto|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|en|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|en|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|en|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * **Kinosaki Onsen Festival** (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * **Kinosaki Dream Plaza** (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * **Kinosaki Danjiri Festival** (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|en|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|en|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|en|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|en|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === **Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):** * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === **Cultural Properties:** * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). **Natural Monuments:** * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|en|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|en|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|en|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]]

In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. The {{ill|Honden|en|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|en|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|en|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === **Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):** * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === **Cultural Properties:** * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). **Natural Monuments:** * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|en|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|en|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|en|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]]

Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * **Kinosaki Onsen Festival** (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * **Kinosaki Dream Plaza** (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * **Kinosaki Danjiri Festival** (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === **Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):** * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === **Cultural Properties:** * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). **Natural Monuments:** * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]]

[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]]

Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |Location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Shisho Shrine |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho {{Shrine |Name = Shisho Shrine |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ {{Shrine |Name = Shisho Shrine |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted [[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine {{Shrine |Name = Shisho Shrine |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]

[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted {{Shrine |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted {{Shrine |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ {{Shrine |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Unsorted completion {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Unsorted completion
