{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier 5]] Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier 6]] Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]

[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier 6]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier 6]] revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier 6]] Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}.== See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}.== See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also! == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Shisho Shrine revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier 6]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) [[Category:Completion tier 6]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神|12=draft}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|12=draft}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市|12=draft}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|12=draft}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|12=draft}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅|12=draft}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|12=draft}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|12=draft}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|12=draft}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|12=draft}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿|12=draft}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|12=draft}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延|12=draft}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|12=draft}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安|12=draft}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|12=draft}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)}}

'''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|12=draft}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神|12=draft}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|12=draft}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市|12=draft}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|12=draft}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|12=draft}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅|12=draft}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|12=draft}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|12=draft}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|12=draft}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|12=draft}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿|12=draft}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|12=draft}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延|12=draft}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|12=draft}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安|12=draft}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|12=draft}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)}}
