{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神|12=draft}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|12=draft}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市|12=draft}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|12=draft}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|12=draft}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅|12=draft}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|12=draft}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|12=draft}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|12=draft}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|12=draft}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿|12=draft}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|12=draft}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延|12=draft}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|12=draft}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安|12=draft}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|12=draft}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)}}

The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|12=draft|12=draft}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安|1=Ōan|lt=Ōan era|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|12=draft|12=draft}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|宗像三女神|" to "|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市|12=draft|12=draft}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|12=draft|12=draft}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|12=draft|12=draft}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=draft|12=draft}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|12=draft|12=draft}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|12=draft|12=draft}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震|12=draft|12=draft}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿|12=draft|12=draft}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延|12=draft|12=draft}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|12=draft|12=draft}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安|1=Ōan|lt=Ōan era|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|12=draft|12=draft}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|12=draft|12=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)}}

In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)}}

revision.comment: 22 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:四所神社 (豊岡市) up to rev 96678754
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 四所神社 |画像 = [[画像:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
神門、拝殿 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]城崎町湯島447 |位置 = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[湯山主大神]] |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝[[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]） |本殿 = 三間社流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月15日]] |神事 = 城崎だんじり祭り（10月14日-15日）
温泉祭り(4月23日) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=四所神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]にある[[神社]]。[[城崎温泉]]の[[外湯]]の一つ「御所の湯」の隣に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == '''湯山主神'''（ゆやまぬしのかみ）を主祭神に、[[多紀理毘売命|多岐里比売神（たきりひめのかみ）]]、[[多岐都比売命|田記津比売神（たきつひめのかみ）]]、[[市杵島比売命|市杵比売神（いちきひめのかみ）]]の[[宗像三女神]]を配し合わせて四所四神を祀る。いずれも[[温泉]]の[[守護神]]として崇められ、また[[宗像三女神]]は水の守護神でもある。 == 由緒 == [[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]）に日生下権守という者が夢中に現れた4柱の神の[[神託]]に従って創建した。[[養老]]元年（[[717年]]）には当社に参拝した[[道智]]に神託があり、外湯「まんだら湯」を開湯したと伝わる。以来、城崎温泉の守護神として、道智開基の[[温泉寺 (豊岡市)|温泉寺]]を[[別当寺]]として崇敬を集めたが、[[明治]]の[[神仏判然令]]で明治3年（[[1870年]]）に同寺と分離し、同6年に[[村社]]に列した。 本殿裏から名水「延命水」が湧出する。 == 祭事 == * [[城崎温泉祭り]] - [[4月23日]]～[[4月24日]]　城崎温泉の開祖である道智上人の開山忌を祈念して行われる。 *城崎温泉夢広場 - [[7月23日]]～[[8月25日]]（お盆・土日祝日を除く20：00～21：00）神社の境内で、スーパーボールすくいなどの屋台やパフォーマンスショーなどのイベントが行われる。また、同期間中、21時から5分間、約200発の夢花火が打ち上げられる。（[[8月10日]]～[[8月16日]]を除く。） *[[城崎だんじり祭り]]（例祭） - [[10月14日]]（宵宮）～[[10月15日]]（本宮） == 社殿 == 現在の社殿は、[[大正]]14年（[[1925年]]）の[[北但馬地震]]で古記録と共に全壊、焼失したため、[[昭和]]3年（[[1928年]]）に再建されたもの。[[本殿]]は[[流造|三間社流造]]、[[拝殿]]は[[入母屋造]]平屋建で共に県の有形文化財。昭和56年（[[1981年]]）に[[和田政春]]の設計、施工による改修が行われている。因みに旧本殿は[[寛延]]4年（[[1751年]]）の建立にかかる総欅造りの広壮なものであった。 == 文化財 == === 兵庫県指定 === 登録有形文化財建造物 * 本殿（[[平成]]18年（[[2006年]]）12月22日登録） * 拝殿（同上） === 豊岡市指定 === 文化財建造物 * 弁天山宝篋印塔「越中次郎兵衛の墓」1基 - [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]の[[応安]]2年（[[1369年]]）の建立（昭和50年3月1日指定） 天然記念物 * [[ヒメハルゼミ]]の発生の地（平成2年5月18日指定） == 交通 == * [[山陰本線]] [[城崎温泉駅]] 徒歩15分 == 関連項目 == * [[四所神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html 四所神社]（兵庫県神社庁） * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html 温泉寺、四所神社、極楽寺]（城崎温泉観光協会） {{神道 横}} {{Kinosaki onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししよしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:温泉神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]]

{{神社 |名称 = 四所神社 |画像 = [[画像:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
神門、拝殿 |所在地 = [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]城崎町湯島447 |位置 = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[湯山主大神]] |社格 = 旧村社 |創建 = 伝[[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]） |本殿 = 三間社流造 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月15日]] |神事 = 城崎だんじり祭り（10月14日-15日）
温泉祭り(4月23日) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=四所神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''四所神社'''（ししょじんじゃ）は[[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]にある[[神社]]。[[城崎温泉]]の[[外湯]]の一つ「御所の湯」の隣に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == '''湯山主神'''（ゆやまぬしのかみ）を主祭神に、[[多紀理毘売命|多岐里比売神（たきりひめのかみ）]]、[[多岐都比売命|田記津比売神（たきつひめのかみ）]]、[[市杵島比売命|市杵比売神（いちきひめのかみ）]]の[[宗像三女神]]を配し合わせて四所四神を祀る。いずれも[[温泉]]の[[守護神]]として崇められ、また[[宗像三女神]]は水の守護神でもある。 == 由緒 == [[和銅]]元年（[[708年]]）に日生下権守という者が夢中に現れた4柱の神の[[神託]]に従って創建した。[[養老]]元年（[[717年]]）には当社に参拝した[[道智]]に神託があり、外湯「まんだら湯」を開湯したと伝わる。以来、城崎温泉の守護神として、道智開基の[[温泉寺 (豊岡市)|温泉寺]]を[[別当寺]]として崇敬を集めたが、[[明治]]の[[神仏判然令]]で明治3年（[[1870年]]）に同寺と分離し、同6年に[[村社]]に列した。 本殿裏から名水「延命水」が湧出する。 == 祭事 == * [[城崎温泉祭り]] - [[4月23日]]～[[4月24日]]　城崎温泉の開祖である道智上人の開山忌を祈念して行われる。 *城崎温泉夢広場 - [[7月23日]]～[[8月25日]]（お盆・土日祝日を除く20：00～21：00）神社の境内で、スーパーボールすくいなどの屋台やパフォーマンスショーなどのイベントが行われる。また、同期間中、21時から5分間、約200発の夢花火が打ち上げられる。（[[8月10日]]～[[8月16日]]を除く。） *[[城崎だんじり祭り]]（例祭） - [[10月14日]]（宵宮）～[[10月15日]]（本宮） == 社殿 == 現在の社殿は、[[大正]]14年（[[1925年]]）の[[北但馬地震]]で古記録と共に全壊、焼失したため、[[昭和]]3年（[[1928年]]）に再建されたもの。[[本殿]]は[[流造|三間社流造]]、[[拝殿]]は[[入母屋造]]平屋建で共に県の有形文化財。昭和56年（[[1981年]]）に[[和田政春]]の設計、施工による改修が行われている。因みに旧本殿は[[寛延]]4年（[[1751年]]）の建立にかかる総欅造りの広壮なものであった。 == 文化財 == === 兵庫県指定 === 登録有形文化財建造物 * 本殿（[[平成]]18年（[[2006年]]）12月22日登録） * 拝殿（同上） === 豊岡市指定 === 文化財建造物 * 弁天山宝篋印塔「越中次郎兵衛の墓」1基 - [[南北朝時代 (日本)|南北朝時代]]の[[応安]]2年（[[1369年]]）の建立（昭和50年3月1日指定） 天然記念物 * [[ヒメハルゼミ]]の発生の地（平成2年5月18日指定） == 交通 == * [[山陰本線]] [[城崎温泉駅]] 徒歩15分 == 関連項目 == * [[四所神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html 四所神社]（兵庫県神社庁） * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html 温泉寺、四所神社、極楽寺]（城崎温泉観光協会） {{神道 横}} {{Kinosaki onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ししよしんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:豊岡市の建築物]] [[Category:豊岡市の歴史]] [[Category:温泉神社]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧村社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安|1=Ōan|lt=Ōan era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyogo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyogo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyogo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyogo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安|1=Ōan|lt=Ōan era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyogo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|qq=}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyooka, Hyogo|qq=}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshono-yu|qq=}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|qq=}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō era|qq=}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō era|qq=}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|qq=}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandara-yu|qq=}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|qq=}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|qq=}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1925 North Tajima Earthquake|qq=}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honden|qq=}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|qq=}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-zukuri|qq=}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyōgo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'en era|qq=}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyōgo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Jirōbei|qq=}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanboku-chō period|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安|1=Ōan|lt=Ōan era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōan era|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōan|qq=}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Euterpnosia chibensis|qq=}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanin Main Line|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]]

revision.comment: Bot: merge duplicate translation from [[Shisho Shrine (Toyooka City)]] into [[Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]]; accidental double translation

'''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Euterpnosia chibensis|qq=}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanin Main Line|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Shinto Shrine | name = Shisho Shrine (四所神社) | image = {{ill|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|ja|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=}}
Shinmon, Haiden | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Toyooka City|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyooka City|qq=}}城崎町湯島447 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|qq=}} | type = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | founded = Legendary{{ill|Wado|ja|和銅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wado|qq=}} 1st year ({{ill|708|ja|708|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/708|qq=}}) | main_shrine = Sanmen-sha style construction (三間社流造) | alternative_names = | markers = | festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 15|qq=}} | religious_events = {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|qq=}} (October 14–15)
{{ill|Onsen Festival|ja|温泉祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Festival|qq=}} (April 23) | map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine (四所神社)|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | image = File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Toyooka City|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyooka City|qq=}}. It is situated adjacent to the public bath "Gosho no Yu", one of the baths of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}. == Deity == '''{{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|qq=}}''' (ゆやまぬしのかみ) is enshrined as the principal deity, accompanied by the deities {{ill|Takikuri-hime-no-kami|ja|多紀理毘売命|lt=Takikuri-hime (多岐里比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikuri-hime-no-kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-kami|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Takitsuhime (田記津比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-kami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|ja|市杵島比売命|lt=Ichikihime (市杵比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|qq=}} of the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, thus enshrining the Four Shrine Gods. All are revered as guardian deities of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} are additionally venerated as water guardian deities. == History == In the 1st year of {{ill|Wado|ja|和銅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wado|qq=}} ({{ill|708|ja|708|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/708|qq=}}), a man named Nichisei Shitagenokami founded the shrine in accordance with a divine oracle ({{ill|Divine Oracle|ja|神託|1=Oracle|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Oracle|13=User:Immanuelle/Oracle|qq=}}) delivered by four deities in a dream. In the 1st year of {{ill|Yoro|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro|qq=}} ({{ill|717|ja|717|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/717|qq=}}), a divine oracle was received by {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|qq=}}, who visited the shrine, and he is said to have inaugurated the public bath "Mandara-yu". Since then, the shrine has been venerated as the guardian of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, with the Onsen-ji temple established by Dōchi ({{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺 (豊岡市)|lt=Onsen Temple|lt_ja=温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|qq=}}) serving as its head temple. However, under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|ja|神仏判然令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|qq=}} of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} era, the shrine separated from that temple in the 3rd year ({{ill|1870|ja|1870|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=}}) and was reclassified as a village shrine in the 6th year. A renowned spring, "Enmei-sui", issues forth from behind the main shrine. == Festivals == * {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Festival|ja|城崎温泉祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Festival|qq=}} – {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 23|qq=}} to {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24|qq=}}: Held in commemoration of the anniversary of the enlightenment of Dōchi Shōnin, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * Kinosaki Onsen Dream Plaza – {{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 23|qq=}} to {{ill|August 25|ja|8月25日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 25|qq=}} (excluding Obon and weekends/holidays, from 20:00 to 21:00): Various events such as super ball scooping, food stalls, and performance shows are held within the shrine precincts. Additionally, during this period, approximately 200 "Dream Fireworks" are launched for 5 minutes beginning at 21:00 (excluding {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 10|qq=}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 16|qq=}}). * {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|qq=}} (festival) – {{ill|October 14|ja|10月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 14|qq=}} (Yoimiya) to {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 15|qq=}} (Main Shrine) == Shrine Buildings == The current shrine buildings were completely destroyed and burned along with ancient records in the 14th year of the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} era ({{ill|1925|ja|1925|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1925|qq=}}) during the {{ill|Kita-Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Tajima Earthquake|qq=}}, and were rebuilt in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} era ({{ill|1928|ja|1928|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1928|qq=}}). The {{ill|Main Shrine|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Shrine|qq=}} is constructed in the Sanmen-sha style, and the {{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worship Hall|qq=}} is a single-story building with a hip-and-gable roof; both are designated as tangible cultural properties of the prefecture. In the 56th year of the Shōwa era ({{ill|1981|ja|1981|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1981|qq=}}), renovations were carried out under the design and construction of {{ill|Wada Masaharu|ja|和田政春|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Masaharu|qq=}}. Incidentally, the former main shrine was an expansive structure built entirely of zelkova (keyaki), dating from the 4th year of the {{ill|Kan'en|ja|寛延|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'en|qq=}} era ({{ill|1751|ja|1751|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1751|qq=}}). == Cultural Properties == === Designated by Hyōgo Prefecture === Registered Tangible Cultural Property Buildings: * {{ill|Main Shrine|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Shrine|qq=}} (registered on December 22, {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 18 ({{ill|2006|ja|2006|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}})) * {{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worship Hall|qq=}} (same as above) === Designated by Toyooka City === Cultural Property Buildings: * Benten-zan Hōkyōintō "Etchu Jirōbee's Grave" (one unit) – Built in the 2nd year of {{ill|Ou'an|ja|応安|1=Ōan|lt=Ōan era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ou'an|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōan|qq=}} during the {{ill|Nanbokuchō period|ja|南北朝時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbokuchō period|qq=}} ({{ill|1369|ja|1369|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1369|qq=}}); designated on March 1, Shōwa 50. Natural Monument: * The birthplace of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeharuzemi|qq=}} (designated May 18, Heisei 2) == Access == * {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanin Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Station|qq=}} – 15-minute walk. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourist Association) {{Shinto}} {{Kinosaki Onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka City]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka City)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[ja:四所神社]] [[Category:Merged pages]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|多紀理毘売命|" to "|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|qq=}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyogo|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyooka, Hyogo|qq=}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshono-yu|qq=}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|qq=}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō era|qq=}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō era|qq=}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|qq=}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandara-yu|qq=}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|qq=}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|qq=}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1925 North Tajima Earthquake|qq=}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honden|qq=}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|qq=}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-zukuri|qq=}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyōgo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'en era|qq=}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyōgo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Jirōbei|qq=}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanboku-chō period|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安|1=Ōan|lt=Ōan era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōan era|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōan|qq=}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Euterpnosia chibensis|qq=}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanin Main Line|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyogo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Shinto Shrine | name = Shisho Shrine (四所神社) | image = {{ill|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|ja|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=}}
Shinmon, Haiden | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Toyooka City|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyooka City|qq=}}城崎町湯島447 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|qq=}} | type = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | founded = Legendary{{ill|Wado|ja|和銅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wado|qq=}} 1st year ({{ill|708|ja|708|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/708|qq=}}) | main_shrine = Sanmen-sha style construction (三間社流造) | alternative_names = | markers = | festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 15|qq=}} | religious_events = {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|qq=}} (October 14–15)
{{ill|Onsen Festival|ja|温泉祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Festival|qq=}} (April 23) | map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine (四所神社)|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | image = File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Toyooka City|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyooka City|qq=}}. It is situated adjacent to the public bath "Gosho no Yu", one of the baths of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}. == Deity == '''{{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|qq=}}''' (ゆやまぬしのかみ) is enshrined as the principal deity, accompanied by the deities {{ill|Takikuri-hime-no-kami|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|lt=Takikuri-hime (多岐里比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikuri-hime-no-kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-kami|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Takitsuhime (田記津比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-kami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|ja|市杵島比売命|lt=Ichikihime (市杵比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|qq=}} of the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, thus enshrining the Four Shrine Gods. All are revered as guardian deities of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} are additionally venerated as water guardian deities. == History == In the 1st year of {{ill|Wado|ja|和銅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wado|qq=}} ({{ill|708|ja|708|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/708|qq=}}), a man named Nichisei Shitagenokami founded the shrine in accordance with a divine oracle ({{ill|Divine Oracle|ja|神託|1=Oracle|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Oracle|13=User:Immanuelle/Oracle|qq=}}) delivered by four deities in a dream. In the 1st year of {{ill|Yoro|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro|qq=}} ({{ill|717|ja|717|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/717|qq=}}), a divine oracle was received by {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|qq=}}, who visited the shrine, and he is said to have inaugurated the public bath "Mandara-yu". Since then, the shrine has been venerated as the guardian of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, with the Onsen-ji temple established by Dōchi ({{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺 (豊岡市)|lt=Onsen Temple|lt_ja=温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|qq=}}) serving as its head temple. However, under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|ja|神仏判然令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|qq=}} of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} era, the shrine separated from that temple in the 3rd year ({{ill|1870|ja|1870|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=}}) and was reclassified as a village shrine in the 6th year. A renowned spring, "Enmei-sui", issues forth from behind the main shrine. == Festivals == * {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Festival|ja|城崎温泉祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Festival|qq=}} – {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 23|qq=}} to {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24|qq=}}: Held in commemoration of the anniversary of the enlightenment of Dōchi Shōnin, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * Kinosaki Onsen Dream Plaza – {{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 23|qq=}} to {{ill|August 25|ja|8月25日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 25|qq=}} (excluding Obon and weekends/holidays, from 20:00 to 21:00): Various events such as super ball scooping, food stalls, and performance shows are held within the shrine precincts. Additionally, during this period, approximately 200 "Dream Fireworks" are launched for 5 minutes beginning at 21:00 (excluding {{ill|August 10|ja|8月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 10|qq=}} to {{ill|August 16|ja|8月16日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 16|qq=}}). * {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|qq=}} (festival) – {{ill|October 14|ja|10月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 14|qq=}} (Yoimiya) to {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 15|qq=}} (Main Shrine) == Shrine Buildings == The current shrine buildings were completely destroyed and burned along with ancient records in the 14th year of the {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō|qq=}} era ({{ill|1925|ja|1925|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1925|qq=}}) during the {{ill|Kita-Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kita-Tajima Earthquake|qq=}}, and were rebuilt in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} era ({{ill|1928|ja|1928|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1928|qq=}}). The {{ill|Main Shrine|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Shrine|qq=}} is constructed in the Sanmen-sha style, and the {{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worship Hall|qq=}} is a single-story building with a hip-and-gable roof; both are designated as tangible cultural properties of the prefecture. In the 56th year of the Shōwa era ({{ill|1981|ja|1981|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1981|qq=}}), renovations were carried out under the design and construction of {{ill|Wada Masaharu|ja|和田政春|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Masaharu|qq=}}. Incidentally, the former main shrine was an expansive structure built entirely of zelkova (keyaki), dating from the 4th year of the {{ill|Kan'en|ja|寛延|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'en|qq=}} era ({{ill|1751|ja|1751|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1751|qq=}}). == Cultural Properties == === Designated by Hyōgo Prefecture === Registered Tangible Cultural Property Buildings: * {{ill|Main Shrine|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Shrine|qq=}} (registered on December 22, {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 18 ({{ill|2006|ja|2006|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2006|qq=}})) * {{ill|Worship Hall|ja|拝殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Worship Hall|qq=}} (same as above) === Designated by Toyooka City === Cultural Property Buildings: * Benten-zan Hōkyōintō "Etchu Jirōbee's Grave" (one unit) – Built in the 2nd year of {{ill|Ou'an|ja|応安|1=Ōan|lt=Ōan era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ou'an|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōan|qq=}} during the {{ill|Nanbokuchō period|ja|南北朝時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbokuchō period|qq=}} ({{ill|1369|ja|1369|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1369|qq=}}); designated on March 1, Shōwa 50. Natural Monument: * The birthplace of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeharuzemi|qq=}} (designated May 18, Heisei 2) == Access == * {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanin Main Line|qq=}} {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Station|qq=}} – 15-minute walk. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyogo Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourist Association) {{Shinto}} {{Kinosaki Onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka City]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka City)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[ja:四所神社]] [[Category:Merged pages]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|qq=}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyooka, Hyōgo|qq=}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshono-yu|qq=}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|qq=}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō era|ja|和銅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wadō era|qq=}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō era|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō era|qq=}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|qq=}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandara-yu|qq=}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|qq=}} in the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|qq=}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima Earthquake|ja|北但馬地震|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1925 North Tajima Earthquake|qq=}}. The {{ill|Honden|ja|本殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honden|qq=}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|qq=}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-zukuri|qq=}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyōgo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en era|ja|寛延|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'en era|qq=}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyōgo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Jirōbei|qq=}}, established in the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|ja|南北朝時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanboku-chō period|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōan era|ja|応安|1=Ōan|lt=Ōan era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōan era|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōan|qq=}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Euterpnosia chibensis|qq=}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|ja|城崎温泉駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Sanin Main Line|ja|山陰本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanin Main Line|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Shinto Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Hot Spring Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties in Japan]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[en:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Shinto Shrine | name = Shisho Shrine (四所神社) | image = {{ill|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|ja|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=}}
Shinmon, Haiden | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Toyooka City|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyooka City|qq=}}城崎町湯島447 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|qq=}} | type = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | founded = Legendary{{ill|Wado|ja|和銅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wado|qq=}} 1st year ({{ill|708|ja|708|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/708|qq=}}) | main_shrine = Sanmen-sha style construction (三間社流造) | alternative_names = | markers = | festival = {{ill|October 15|ja|10月15日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 15|qq=}} | religious_events = {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|qq=}} (October 14–15)
{{ill|Onsen Festival|ja|温泉祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Festival|qq=}} (April 23) | map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine (四所神社)|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | image = File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto Shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Toyooka City|ja|豊岡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyooka City|qq=}}. It is situated adjacent to the public bath "Gosho no Yu", one of the baths of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}. == Deity == '''{{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|qq=}}''' (ゆやまぬしのかみ) is enshrined as the principal deity, accompanied by the deities {{ill|Takikuri-hime-no-kami|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|lt=Takikuri-hime (多岐里比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikuri-hime-no-kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-kami|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Takitsuhime (田記津比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-kami|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|ja|市杵島比売命|lt=Ichikihime (市杵比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|qq=}} of the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, thus enshrining the Four Shrine Gods. All are revered as guardian deities of {{ill|Onsen|ja|温泉|1=Onsen|1=Onsen|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} are additionally venerated as water guardian deities. == History == In the 1st year of {{ill|Wado|ja|和銅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wado|qq=}} ({{ill|708|ja|708|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/708|qq=}}), a man named Nichisei Shitagenokami founded the shrine in accordance with a divine oracle ({{ill|Divine Oracle|ja|神託|1=Oracle|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Oracle|13=User:Immanuelle/Oracle|qq=}}) delivered by four deities in a dream. In the 1st year of {{ill|Yoro|ja|養老|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoro|qq=}} ({{ill|717|ja|717|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/717|qq=}}), a divine oracle was received by {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|qq=}}, who visited the shrine, and he is said to have inaugurated the public bath "Mandara-yu". Since then, the shrine has been venerated as the guardian of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, with the Onsen-ji temple established by Dōchi ({{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺 (豊岡市)|lt=Onsen Temple|lt_ja=温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|qq=}}) serving as its head temple. However, under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|ja|神仏判然令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|qq=}} of the {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} era, the shrine separated from that temple in the 3rd year ({{ill|1870|ja|1870|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1870|qq=}}) and was reclassified as a village shrine in the 6th year. A renowned spring, "Enmei-sui", issues forth from behind the main shrine. == Festivals == * {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Festival|ja|城崎温泉祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Festival|qq=}} – {{ill|April 23|ja|4月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 23|qq=}} to {{ill|April 24|ja|4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24|qq=}}: Held in commemoration of the anniversary of the enlightenment of Dōchi Shōnin, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * Kinosaki Onsen Dream Plaza – {{ill|July 23|ja|7月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 23|qq=}} to {{ill|August 25|ja|8月25日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/August 25|qq=}} (excluding Obon and weekends/holidays, from 20:00 to 21:00): Various events such as super ball scooping, food stalls, and performance shows are held within the shrine precincts.
