revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshono-yu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō era]] 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō era]] 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandara-yu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|qq=}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the [[:en:1925 North Tajima earthquake|1925 North Tajima Earthquake]]. The [[:en:Honden|Honden]] is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the worship hall is an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyōgo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in [[:en:Kan'en|Kan'en era]] 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyōgo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Jirōbei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, established in the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], [[:en:Ōan|Ōan era]] 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Euterpnosia chibensis|qq=}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kinosaki Onsen Station|Kinosaki Onsen Station]] on the [[:en:San'in Main Line|Sanin Main Line]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Shinto Shrine | name = Shisho Shrine (四所神社) | image = {{ill|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|ja|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Shinmon, Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka City]]城崎町湯島447 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | founded = Legendary[[:en:Wadō (era)|Wado]] 1st year ([[:en:708|708]]) | main_shrine = Sanmen-sha style construction (三間社流造) | alternative_names = | markers = | festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] | religious_events = {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October 14–15)
{{ill|Onsen Festival|ja|温泉祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (April 23) | map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine (四所神社)|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | image = File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka City]]. It is situated adjacent to the public bath "Gosho no Yu", one of the baths of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}. == Deity == '''{{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (ゆやまぬしのかみ) is enshrined as the principal deity, accompanied by the deities {{ill|Takikuri-hime-no-kami|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|lt=Takikuri-hime (多岐里比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikuri-hime-no-kami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-kami|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Takitsuhime (田記津比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|ja|市杵島比売命|lt=Ichikihime (市杵比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} of the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, thus enshrining the Four Shrine Gods. All are revered as guardian deities of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], and the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} are additionally venerated as water guardian deities. == History == In the 1st year of [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wado]] ([[:en:708|708]]), a man named Nichisei Shitagenokami founded the shrine in accordance with a divine oracle ([[:en:Oracle|Oracle]]) delivered by four deities in a dream. In the 1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yoro]] ([[:en:717|717]]), a divine oracle was received by {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who visited the shrine, and he is said to have inaugurated the public bath "Mandara-yu". Since then, the shrine has been venerated as the guardian of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, with the Onsen-ji temple established by Dōchi ({{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺 (豊岡市)|lt=Onsen Temple|lt_ja=温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|qq=}}) serving as its head temple. However, under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|ja|神仏判然令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, the shrine separated from that temple in the 3rd year ([[:en:1870|1870]]) and was reclassified as a village shrine in the 6th year. A renowned spring, "Enmei-sui", issues forth from behind the main shrine. == Festivals == * {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Festival|ja|城崎温泉祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:April 23|April 23]] to [[:en:April 24|April 24]]: Held in commemoration of the anniversary of the enlightenment of Dōchi Shōnin, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * Kinosaki Onsen Dream Plaza – [[:en:July 23|July 23]] to [[:en:August 25|August 25]] (excluding Obon and weekends/holidays, from 20:00 to 21:00): Various events such as super ball scooping, food stalls, and performance shows are held within the shrine precincts. Additionally, during this period, approximately 200 "Dream Fireworks" are launched for 5 minutes beginning at 21:00 (excluding [[:en:August 10|August 10]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]). * {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (festival) – [[:en:October 14|October 14]] (Yoimiya) to [[:en:October 15|October 15]] (Main Shrine) == Shrine Buildings == The current shrine buildings were completely destroyed and burned along with ancient records in the 14th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era ([[:en:1925|1925]]) during the [[:en:1925 North Tajima earthquake|Kita-Tajima Earthquake]], and were rebuilt in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era ([[:en:1928|1928]]). The [[:en:Honden|Main Shrine]] is constructed in the Sanmen-sha style, and the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]] is a single-story building with a hip-and-gable roof; both are designated as tangible cultural properties of the prefecture. In the 56th year of the Shōwa era ([[:en:1981|1981]]), renovations were carried out under the design and construction of {{ill|Wada Masaharu|ja|和田政春|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Masaharu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Incidentally, the former main shrine was an expansive structure built entirely of zelkova (keyaki), dating from the 4th year of the [[:en:Kan'en|Kan'en]] era ([[:en:1751|1751]]). == Cultural Properties == === Designated by Hyōgo Prefecture === Registered Tangible Cultural Property Buildings: * [[:en:Honden|Main Shrine]] (registered on December 22, [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 18 ([[:en:2006|2006]])) * [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]] (same as above) === Designated by Toyooka City === Cultural Property Buildings: * Benten-zan Hōkyōintō "Etchu Jirōbee's Grave" (one unit) – Built in the 2nd year of [[:en:Ōan|Ōan era]] during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanbokuchō period]] ([[:en:1369|1369]]); designated on March 1, Shōwa 50. Natural Monument: * The birthplace of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeharuzemi|qq=}} (designated May 18, Heisei 2) == Access == * [[:en:San'in Main Line|Sanin Main Line]] [[:en:Kinosaki Onsen Station|Kinosaki Onsen Station]] – 15-minute walk. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourist Association) {{Shinto}} {{Kinosaki Onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka City)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[ja:四所神社]] [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] {{Commons category|Sisho-jinja}}

[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshono-yu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō era]] 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō era]] 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandara-yu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|qq=}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the [[:en:1925 North Tajima earthquake|1925 North Tajima Earthquake]]. The [[:en:Honden|Honden]] is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the worship hall is an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyōgo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in [[:en:Kan'en|Kan'en era]] 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyōgo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Jirōbei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, established in the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], [[:en:Ōan|Ōan era]] 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Euterpnosia chibensis|qq=}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kinosaki Onsen Station|Kinosaki Onsen Station]] on the [[:en:San'in Main Line|Sanin Main Line]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Shinto Shrine | name = Shisho Shrine (四所神社) | image = {{ill|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|ja|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Shinmon, Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka City]]城崎町湯島447 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | founded = Legendary[[:en:Wadō (era)|Wado]] 1st year ([[:en:708|708]]) | main_shrine = Sanmen-sha style construction (三間社流造) | alternative_names = | markers = | festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] | religious_events = {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October 14–15)
{{ill|Onsen Festival|ja|温泉祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (April 23) | map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine (四所神社)|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | image = File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka City]]. It is situated adjacent to the public bath "Gosho no Yu", one of the baths of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}. == Deity == '''{{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (ゆやまぬしのかみ) is enshrined as the principal deity, accompanied by the deities {{ill|Takikuri-hime-no-kami|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|lt=Takikuri-hime (多岐里比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikuri-hime-no-kami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-kami|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Takitsuhime (田記津比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|ja|市杵島比売命|lt=Ichikihime (市杵比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} of the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, thus enshrining the Four Shrine Gods. All are revered as guardian deities of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], and the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} are additionally venerated as water guardian deities. == History == In the 1st year of [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wado]] ([[:en:708|708]]), a man named Nichisei Shitagenokami founded the shrine in accordance with a divine oracle ([[:en:Oracle|Oracle]]) delivered by four deities in a dream. In the 1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yoro]] ([[:en:717|717]]), a divine oracle was received by {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who visited the shrine, and he is said to have inaugurated the public bath "Mandara-yu". Since then, the shrine has been venerated as the guardian of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, with the Onsen-ji temple established by Dōchi ({{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺 (豊岡市)|lt=Onsen Temple|lt_ja=温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|qq=}}) serving as its head temple. However, under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|ja|神仏判然令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, the shrine separated from that temple in the 3rd year ([[:en:1870|1870]]) and was reclassified as a village shrine in the 6th year. A renowned spring, "Enmei-sui", issues forth from behind the main shrine. == Festivals == * {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Festival|ja|城崎温泉祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:April 23|April 23]] to [[:en:April 24|April 24]]: Held in commemoration of the anniversary of the enlightenment of Dōchi Shōnin, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * Kinosaki Onsen Dream Plaza – [[:en:July 23|July 23]] to [[:en:August 25|August 25]] (excluding Obon and weekends/holidays, from 20:00 to 21:00): Various events such as super ball scooping, food stalls, and performance shows are held within the shrine precincts. Additionally, during this period, approximately 200 "Dream Fireworks" are launched for 5 minutes beginning at 21:00 (excluding [[:en:August 10|August 10]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]). * {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (festival) – [[:en:October 14|October 14]] (Yoimiya) to [[:en:October 15|October 15]] (Main Shrine) == Shrine Buildings == The current shrine buildings were completely destroyed and burned along with ancient records in the 14th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era ([[:en:1925|1925]]) during the [[:en:1925 North Tajima earthquake|Kita-Tajima Earthquake]], and were rebuilt in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era ([[:en:1928|1928]]). The [[:en:Honden|Main Shrine]] is constructed in the Sanmen-sha style, and the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]] is a single-story building with a hip-and-gable roof; both are designated as tangible cultural properties of the prefecture. In the 56th year of the Shōwa era ([[:en:1981|1981]]), renovations were carried out under the design and construction of {{ill|Wada Masaharu|ja|和田政春|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Masaharu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Incidentally, the former main shrine was an expansive structure built entirely of zelkova (keyaki), dating from the 4th year of the [[:en:Kan'en|Kan'en]] era ([[:en:1751|1751]]). == Cultural Properties == === Designated by Hyōgo Prefecture === Registered Tangible Cultural Property Buildings: * [[:en:Honden|Main Shrine]] (registered on December 22, [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 18 ([[:en:2006|2006]])) * [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]] (same as above) === Designated by Toyooka City === Cultural Property Buildings: * Benten-zan Hōkyōintō "Etchu Jirōbee's Grave" (one unit) – Built in the 2nd year of [[:en:Ōan|Ōan era]] during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanbokuchō period]] ([[:en:1369|1369]]); designated on March 1, Shōwa 50. Natural Monument: * The birthplace of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeharuzemi|qq=}} (designated May 18, Heisei 2) == Access == * [[:en:San'in Main Line|Sanin Main Line]] [[:en:Kinosaki Onsen Station|Kinosaki Onsen Station]] – 15-minute walk. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourist Association) {{Shinto}} {{Kinosaki Onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka City)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[ja:四所神社]] [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] {{Commons category|Sisho-jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11419885]] Wikidata name: Shisho Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|250px]]
Shrine Gate and Worship Hall |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshono-yu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō era]] 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō era]] 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandara-yu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|qq=}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the [[:en:1925 North Tajima earthquake|1925 North Tajima Earthquake]]. The [[:en:Honden|Honden]] is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the worship hall is an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyōgo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in [[:en:Kan'en|Kan'en era]] 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyōgo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Jirōbei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, established in the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], [[:en:Ōan|Ōan era]] 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Euterpnosia chibensis|qq=}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kinosaki Onsen Station|Kinosaki Onsen Station]] on the [[:en:San'in Main Line|Sanin Main Line]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| | name = Shisho Shrine (四所神社) | image = {{ill|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|ja|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Shinmon, Haiden | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka City]]城崎町湯島447 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | founded = Legendary[[:en:Wadō (era)|Wado]] 1st year ([[:en:708|708]]) | main_shrine = Sanmen-sha style construction (三間社流造) | alternative_names = | markers = | festival = [[:en:October 15|October 15]] | religious_events = {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October 14–15)
{{ill|Onsen Festival|ja|温泉祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (April 23) | map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine (四所神社)|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | image = File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]][[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka City]]. It is situated adjacent to the public bath "Gosho no Yu", one of the baths of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}. == Deity == '''{{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (ゆやまぬしのかみ) is enshrined as the principal deity, accompanied by the deities {{ill|Takikuri-hime-no-kami|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|lt=Takikuri-hime (多岐里比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikuri-hime-no-kami|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-kami|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Takitsuhime (田記津比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|ja|市杵島比売命|lt=Ichikihime (市杵比売神)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} of the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, thus enshrining the Four Shrine Gods. All are revered as guardian deities of [[:en:Onsen|Onsen]], and the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} are additionally venerated as water guardian deities. == History == In the 1st year of [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wado]] ([[:en:708|708]]), a man named Nichisei Shitagenokami founded the shrine in accordance with a divine oracle ([[:en:Oracle|Oracle]]) delivered by four deities in a dream. In the 1st year of [[:en:Yōrō|Yoro]] ([[:en:717|717]]), a divine oracle was received by {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who visited the shrine, and he is said to have inaugurated the public bath "Mandara-yu". Since then, the shrine has been venerated as the guardian of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, with the Onsen-ji temple established by Dōchi ({{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺 (豊岡市)|lt=Onsen Temple|lt_ja=温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|qq=}}) serving as its head temple. However, under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|ja|神仏判然令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] era, the shrine separated from that temple in the 3rd year ([[:en:1870|1870]]) and was reclassified as a village shrine in the 6th year. A renowned spring, "Enmei-sui", issues forth from behind the main shrine. == Festivals == * {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Festival|ja|城崎温泉祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – [[:en:April 23|April 23]] to [[:en:April 24|April 24]]: Held in commemoration of the anniversary of the enlightenment of Dōchi Shōnin, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * Kinosaki Onsen Dream Plaza – [[:en:July 23|July 23]] to [[:en:August 25|August 25]] (excluding Obon and weekends/holidays, from 20:00 to 21:00): Various events such as super ball scooping, food stalls, and performance shows are held within the shrine precincts. Additionally, during this period, approximately 200 "Dream Fireworks" are launched for 5 minutes beginning at 21:00 (excluding [[:en:August 10|August 10]] to [[:en:August 16|August 16]]). * {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (festival) – [[:en:October 14|October 14]] (Yoimiya) to [[:en:October 15|October 15]] (Main Shrine) == Shrine Buildings == The current shrine buildings were completely destroyed and burned along with ancient records in the 14th year of the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] era ([[:en:1925|1925]]) during the [[:en:1925 North Tajima earthquake|Kita-Tajima Earthquake]], and were rebuilt in the 3rd year of the [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] era ([[:en:1928|1928]]). The [[:en:Honden|Main Shrine]] is constructed in the Sanmen-sha style, and the [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]] is a single-story building with a hip-and-gable roof; both are designated as tangible cultural properties of the prefecture. In the 56th year of the Shōwa era ([[:en:1981|1981]]), renovations were carried out under the design and construction of {{ill|Wada Masaharu|ja|和田政春|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Masaharu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Incidentally, the former main shrine was an expansive structure built entirely of zelkova (keyaki), dating from the 4th year of the [[:en:Kan'en|Kan'en]] era ([[:en:1751|1751]]). == Cultural Properties == === Designated by Hyōgo Prefecture === Registered Tangible Cultural Property Buildings: * [[:en:Honden|Main Shrine]] (registered on December 22, [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 18 ([[:en:2006|2006]])) * [[:en:Haiden (Shinto)|Worship Hall]] (same as above) === Designated by Toyooka City === Cultural Property Buildings: * Benten-zan Hōkyōintō "Etchu Jirōbee's Grave" (one unit) – Built in the 2nd year of [[:en:Ōan|Ōan era]] during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanbokuchō period]] ([[:en:1369|1369]]); designated on March 1, Shōwa 50. Natural Monument: * The birthplace of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeharuzemi|qq=}} (designated May 18, Heisei 2) == Access == * [[:en:San'in Main Line|Sanin Main Line]] [[:en:Kinosaki Onsen Station|Kinosaki Onsen Station]] – 15-minute walk. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourist Association) {{Shinto}} {{Kinosaki Onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka City)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[ja:四所神社]] [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[Category:Buildings in Toyooka]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] {{Commons category|Sisho-jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11419885]] Wikidata name: Shisho Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35.626222|N|134.807611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyamanushi no Kami|qid=Q112298533}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |prefecture = {{ill|Toyooka|qid=Q696388}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, [[:en:Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka, Hyōgo]], adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshono-yu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to [[:en:Wadō (era)|Wadō era]] 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In [[:en:Yōrō|Yōrō era]] 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandara-yu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離}} in the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|qq=}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the [[:en:1925 North Tajima earthquake|1925 North Tajima Earthquake]]. The [[:en:Honden|Honden]] is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the worship hall is an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyōgo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in [[:en:Kan'en|Kan'en era]] 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyōgo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Jirōbei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, established in the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō period]], [[:en:Ōan|Ōan era]] 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Euterpnosia chibensis|qq=}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from [[:en:Kinosaki Onsen Station|Kinosaki Onsen Station]] on the [[:en:San'in Main Line|Sanin Main Line]]. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Son-sha]] }} [[sn:Shisho Shrine (Toyooka)]] [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| | name = Shisho Shrine (四所神社) | image = {{ill|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|ja|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
