revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Sisho-jinja]][[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Sisho-jinja]][[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Sisho-jinja]][[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shisho Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg |location = 447 Yushima, Kinosaki-cho, Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|35.626222|N|134.807611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yuyamanushi no Kami|WD=Q112298533|1=Yuyamanushi no Kami}} |Rank = Former Village Shrine |Established = {{Small|Traditionally}} [[Wadō]] 1 (708) |Main Hall = {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Festivals = [[October 15]]
[[Kinosaki Danjiri Festival]] (October 14–15)
[[Hot Spring Festival]] (April 23) |Map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} |location = {{ill|Toyooka|WD=Q696388|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}} }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|en|Toyooka, Hyōgo|WD=Q696388|lt=Toyooka, Hyōgo|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}, adjacent to the {{ill|Goshono-yu|ja|御所の湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshono-yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540751|1=Goshono-yu}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Wadō era|1=Wadō (era)}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō era|1=Yōrō}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandara-yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540745|1=Mandarayu}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|1=Onsen Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562939}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima earthquake|en|1925 North Tajima earthquake|WD=Q11401118|lt=1925 North Tajima Earthquake|1=1925 North Tajima earthquake}}. The {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|WD=Q2044994|lt=Honden|1=Honden}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyōgo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en|en|Kan'en|WD=Q1145723|lt=Kan'en era|1=Kan'en}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyōgo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Jirōbei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, established in the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}}, {{ill|Ōan|en|Ōan|WD=Q1146245|lt=Ōan era|1=Ōan}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Euterpnosia chibensis|1=Euterpnosia chibensis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5976326}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|en|Kinosaki Onsen Station|WD=Q1055689|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} on the {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=Sanin Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| | name = Shisho Shrine (四所神社) | image = {{ill|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|ja|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Shinmon, Haiden | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|en|Toyooka, Hyōgo|WD=Q696388|lt=Toyooka City|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}城崎町湯島447 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | founded = Legendary{{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Wado|1=Wadō (era)}} 1st year ({{ill|708|en|708|WD=Q23511|lt=708|1=708}}) | main_shrine = Sanmen-sha style construction (三間社流造) | alternative_names = | markers = | festival = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} | religious_events = {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October 14–15)
{{ill|Onsen Festival|ja|温泉祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (April 23) | map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine (四所神社)|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | image = File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|en|Toyooka, Hyōgo|WD=Q696388|lt=Toyooka City|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}. It is situated adjacent to the public bath "Gosho no Yu", one of the baths of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}. == Deity == '''{{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (ゆやまぬしのかみ) is enshrined as the principal deity, accompanied by the deities {{ill|Takikuri-hime-no-kami|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|lt=Takikuri-hime (多岐里比売神)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikuri-hime-no-kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-kami|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Takitsuhime (田記津比売神)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ|1=Tagitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932592}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|ja|市杵島比売命|lt=Ichikihime (市杵比売神)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-kami||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} of the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, thus enshrining the Four Shrine Gods. All are revered as guardian deities of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}}, and the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} are additionally venerated as water guardian deities. == History == In the 1st year of {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Wado|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|708|en|708|WD=Q23511|lt=708|1=708}}), a man named Nichisei Shitagenokami founded the shrine in accordance with a divine oracle ({{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=Oracle|1=Oracle}}) delivered by four deities in a dream. In the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yoro|1=Yōrō}} ({{ill|717|en|717|WD=Q23532|lt=717|1=717}}), a divine oracle was received by {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who visited the shrine, and he is said to have inaugurated the public bath "Mandara-yu". Since then, the shrine has been venerated as the guardian of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, with the Onsen-ji temple established by Dōchi ({{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺 (豊岡市)|lt=Onsen Temple|lt_ja=温泉寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|1=Onsen-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562944}}) serving as its head temple. However, under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|ja|神仏判然令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era, the shrine separated from that temple in the 3rd year ({{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}}) and was reclassified as a village shrine in the 6th year. A renowned spring, "Enmei-sui", issues forth from behind the main shrine. == Festivals == * {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Festival|ja|城崎温泉祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|WD=Q2527|lt=April 23|1=April 23}} to {{ill|April 24|en|April 24|WD=Q2529|lt=April 24|1=April 24}}: Held in commemoration of the anniversary of the enlightenment of Dōchi Shōnin, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * Kinosaki Onsen Dream Plaza – {{ill|July 23|en|July 23|WD=Q2722|lt=July 23|1=July 23}} to {{ill|August 25|en|August 25|WD=Q2819|lt=August 25|1=August 25}} (excluding Obon and weekends/holidays, from 20:00 to 21:00): Various events such as super ball scooping, food stalls, and performance shows are held within the shrine precincts. Additionally, during this period, approximately 200 "Dream Fireworks" are launched for 5 minutes beginning at 21:00 (excluding {{ill|August 10|en|August 10|WD=Q2779|lt=August 10|1=August 10}} to {{ill|August 16|en|August 16|WD=Q2812|lt=August 16|1=August 16}}). * {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (festival) – {{ill|October 14|en|October 14|WD=Q2922|lt=October 14|1=October 14}} (Yoimiya) to {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} (Main Shrine) == Shrine Buildings == The current shrine buildings were completely destroyed and burned along with ancient records in the 14th year of the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} era ({{ill|1925|en|1925|WD=Q18107|lt=1925|1=1925}}) during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima earthquake|en|1925 North Tajima earthquake|WD=Q11401118|lt=Kita-Tajima Earthquake|1=1925 North Tajima earthquake}}, and were rebuilt in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} era ({{ill|1928|en|1928|WD=Q19715|lt=1928|1=1928}}). The {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|WD=Q2044994|lt=Main Shrine|1=Honden}} is constructed in the Sanmen-sha style, and the {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Worship Hall|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} is a single-story building with a hip-and-gable roof; both are designated as tangible cultural properties of the prefecture. In the 56th year of the Shōwa era ({{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981|1=1981}}), renovations were carried out under the design and construction of {{ill|Wada Masaharu|ja|和田政春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Masaharu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Incidentally, the former main shrine was an expansive structure built entirely of zelkova (keyaki), dating from the 4th year of the {{ill|Kan'en|en|Kan'en|WD=Q1145723|lt=Kan'en|1=Kan'en}} era ({{ill|1751|en|1751|WD=Q7615|lt=1751|1=1751}}). == Cultural Properties == === Designated by Hyōgo Prefecture === Registered Tangible Cultural Property Buildings: * {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|WD=Q2044994|lt=Main Shrine|1=Honden}} (registered on December 22, {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 18 ({{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}})) * {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Worship Hall|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} (same as above) === Designated by Toyooka City === Cultural Property Buildings: * Benten-zan Hōkyōintō "Etchu Jirōbee's Grave" (one unit) – Built in the 2nd year of {{ill|Ōan|en|Ōan|WD=Q1146245|lt=Ōan era|1=Ōan}} during the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanbokuchō period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}} ({{ill|1369|en|1369|WD=Q6211|lt=1369|1=1369}}); designated on March 1, Shōwa 50. Natural Monument: * The birthplace of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeharuzemi|1=Euterpnosia chibensis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5976326}} (designated May 18, Heisei 2) == Access == * {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=Sanin Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|en|Kinosaki Onsen Station|WD=Q1055689|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} – 15-minute walk. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine|1=Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419883}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourist Association) {{Shinto}} {{Kinosaki Onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[ja:四所神社]] {{Commons category|Sisho-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11419885}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

link invalid|WD=Q135540751|1=Goshono-yu}}, one of the town’s prominent hot springs. == Deities == The shrine enshrines: * {{ill|Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja|湯山主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-nushi no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the primary deity. * The {{ill|Munakata Sanjoshin|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Sanjoshin|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Takiribime|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takiribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}—are also worshipped as protective deities of the hot springs and water. == History == The shrine’s origins trace back to {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Wadō era|1=Wadō (era)}} 1 (708), when a local official named Nisei Shimo Gongen received a divine revelation in a dream from four deities. In {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yōrō era|1=Yōrō}} 1 (717), the monk {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} visited the shrine and received an oracle that led to the establishment of the {{ill|Mandara-yu|ja|まんだら湯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandara-yu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135540745|1=Mandarayu}}, the first hot spring bath in Kinosaki. Since then, the shrine has been revered as the guardian of Kinosaki Onsen. During the {{ill|Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Hanzenrei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} in the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine separated from its affiliated temple, {{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|1=Onsen Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562939}}, and was officially designated a village shrine in 1873. Behind the main hall, a spring known as "Enmei-sui" (Life-Prolonging Water) flows continuously. == Festivals == * '''Kinosaki Onsen Festival''' (April 23–24): Commemorates the memorial day of the monk Dōchi, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * '''Kinosaki Dream Plaza''' (July 23–August 25): Evening festivities, including small booths and performances, are held in the shrine precincts, with nightly fireworks (except August 10–16). * '''Kinosaki Danjiri Festival''' (October 14–15): A traditional festival with portable shrines and floats. == Shrine Structures == The current shrine structures were rebuilt in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 3 (1928) after the original buildings were destroyed during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima earthquake|en|1925 North Tajima earthquake|WD=Q11401118|lt=1925 North Tajima Earthquake|1=1925 North Tajima earthquake}}. The {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|WD=Q2044994|lt=Honden|1=Honden}} is a {{ill|Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja|三間社流造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangensha Nagare-zukuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the worship hall is an {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} structure, both registered as cultural properties of Hyōgo Prefecture. The original main hall, constructed in {{ill|Kan'en|en|Kan'en|WD=Q1145723|lt=Kan'en era|1=Kan'en}} 4 (1751), was made entirely of zelkova wood and was significantly larger. == Cultural Properties == === Hyōgo Prefecture Designations === '''Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Buildings):''' * Main Hall (registered December 22, 2006) * Worship Hall (registered December 22, 2006) === Toyooka City Designations === '''Cultural Properties:''' * Benten-yama Hōkyōintō (Pagoda of Benzaiten Mountain): A grave marker of {{ill|Echū Jirōbei|ja|越中次郎兵衛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Echū Jirōbei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, established in the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}}, {{ill|Ōan|en|Ōan|WD=Q1146245|lt=Ōan era|1=Ōan}} 2 (1369). '''Natural Monuments:''' * Habitat of {{ill|Euterpnosia chibensis|lt=Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|ceb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Euterpnosia chibensis|1=Euterpnosia chibensis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5976326}} (a cicada species). == Access == * 15-minute walk from {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|en|Kinosaki Onsen Station|WD=Q1055689|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} on the {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=Sanin Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}}. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|四所神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Directory) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourism Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| | name = Shisho Shrine (四所神社) | image = {{ill|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|ja|Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
Shinmon, Haiden | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|en|Toyooka, Hyōgo|WD=Q696388|lt=Toyooka City|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}城崎町湯島447 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|37|34.4|N|134|48|27.4|E|scale:25000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | founded = Legendary{{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Wado|1=Wadō (era)}} 1st year ({{ill|708|en|708|WD=Q23511|lt=708|1=708}}) | main_shrine = Sanmen-sha style construction (三間社流造) | alternative_names = | markers = | festival = {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} | religious_events = {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October 14–15)
{{ill|Onsen Festival|ja|温泉祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (April 23) | map = {{Location map|Japan Hyōgo Prefecture|label=Shisho Shrine (四所神社)|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} | image = File:Sisho-jinja Kinosaki01s4592.jpg }} '''Shisho Shrine''' (四所神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|Toyooka, Hyōgo|en|Toyooka, Hyōgo|WD=Q696388|lt=Toyooka City|1=Toyooka, Hyōgo}}. It is situated adjacent to the public bath "Gosho no Yu", one of the baths of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}. == Deity == '''{{ill|Yuyama-no-Okami|ja|湯山主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuyama-no-Okami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' (ゆやまぬしのかみ) is enshrined as the principal deity, accompanied by the deities {{ill|Takikuri-hime-no-kami|ja|多紀理毘売命|1=Tagorihime|2=ja|3=タキリビメ|4=fr|5=Takiribime|6=zh|7=多紀理毘賣命|lt=Takikuri-hime (多岐里比売神)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takikuri-hime-no-kami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, {{ill|Takitsuhime-no-kami|ja|多岐都比売命|lt=Takitsuhime (田記津比売神)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takitsuhime-no-kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ|1=Tagitsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10932592}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-kami|ja|市杵島比売命|lt=Ichikihime (市杵比売神)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-kami||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} of the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}, thus enshrining the Four Shrine Gods. All are revered as guardian deities of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}}, and the {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}} are additionally venerated as water guardian deities. == History == In the 1st year of {{ill|Wadō (era)|en|Wadō (era)|WD=Q1373026|lt=Wado|1=Wadō (era)}} ({{ill|708|en|708|WD=Q23511|lt=708|1=708}}), a man named Nichisei Shitagenokami founded the shrine in accordance with a divine oracle ({{ill|Oracle|en|Oracle|WD=Q217123|lt=Oracle|1=Oracle}}) delivered by four deities in a dream. In the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|WD=Q1373061|lt=Yoro|1=Yōrō}} ({{ill|717|en|717|WD=Q23532|lt=717|1=717}}), a divine oracle was received by {{ill|Dōchi|ja|道智|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who visited the shrine, and he is said to have inaugurated the public bath "Mandara-yu". Since then, the shrine has been venerated as the guardian of {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen|ja|城崎温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen|qq=}}, with the Onsen-ji temple established by Dōchi ({{ill|Onsen-ji|ja|温泉寺 (豊岡市)|lt=Onsen Temple|lt_ja=温泉寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onsen-ji|1=Onsen-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11562944}}) serving as its head temple. However, under the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|ja|神仏判然令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Decree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era, the shrine separated from that temple in the 3rd year ({{ill|1870|en|1870|WD=Q7741|lt=1870|1=1870}}) and was reclassified as a village shrine in the 6th year. A renowned spring, "Enmei-sui", issues forth from behind the main shrine. == Festivals == * {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Festival|ja|城崎温泉祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Onsen Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – {{ill|April 23|en|April 23|WD=Q2527|lt=April 23|1=April 23}} to {{ill|April 24|en|April 24|WD=Q2529|lt=April 24|1=April 24}}: Held in commemoration of the anniversary of the enlightenment of Dōchi Shōnin, the founder of Kinosaki Onsen. * Kinosaki Onsen Dream Plaza – {{ill|July 23|en|July 23|WD=Q2722|lt=July 23|1=July 23}} to {{ill|August 25|en|August 25|WD=Q2819|lt=August 25|1=August 25}} (excluding Obon and weekends/holidays, from 20:00 to 21:00): Various events such as super ball scooping, food stalls, and performance shows are held within the shrine precincts. Additionally, during this period, approximately 200 "Dream Fireworks" are launched for 5 minutes beginning at 21:00 (excluding {{ill|August 10|en|August 10|WD=Q2779|lt=August 10|1=August 10}} to {{ill|August 16|en|August 16|WD=Q2812|lt=August 16|1=August 16}}). * {{ill|Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja|城崎だんじり祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinosaki Danjiri Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (festival) – {{ill|October 14|en|October 14|WD=Q2922|lt=October 14|1=October 14}} (Yoimiya) to {{ill|October 15|en|October 15|WD=Q2919|lt=October 15|1=October 15}} (Main Shrine) == Shrine Buildings == The current shrine buildings were completely destroyed and burned along with ancient records in the 14th year of the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} era ({{ill|1925|en|1925|WD=Q18107|lt=1925|1=1925}}) during the {{ill|1925 North Tajima earthquake|en|1925 North Tajima earthquake|WD=Q11401118|lt=Kita-Tajima Earthquake|1=1925 North Tajima earthquake}}, and were rebuilt in the 3rd year of the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} era ({{ill|1928|en|1928|WD=Q19715|lt=1928|1=1928}}). The {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|WD=Q2044994|lt=Main Shrine|1=Honden}} is constructed in the Sanmen-sha style, and the {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Worship Hall|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} is a single-story building with a hip-and-gable roof; both are designated as tangible cultural properties of the prefecture. In the 56th year of the Shōwa era ({{ill|1981|en|1981|WD=Q2437|lt=1981|1=1981}}), renovations were carried out under the design and construction of {{ill|Wada Masaharu|ja|和田政春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada Masaharu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Incidentally, the former main shrine was an expansive structure built entirely of zelkova (keyaki), dating from the 4th year of the {{ill|Kan'en|en|Kan'en|WD=Q1145723|lt=Kan'en|1=Kan'en}} era ({{ill|1751|en|1751|WD=Q7615|lt=1751|1=1751}}). == Cultural Properties == === Designated by Hyōgo Prefecture === Registered Tangible Cultural Property Buildings: * {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|WD=Q2044994|lt=Main Shrine|1=Honden}} (registered on December 22, {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 18 ({{ill|2006|en|2006|WD=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}})) * {{ill|Haiden (Shinto)|en|Haiden (Shinto)|WD=Q3125745|lt=Worship Hall|1=Haiden (Shinto)}} (same as above) === Designated by Toyooka City === Cultural Property Buildings: * Benten-zan Hōkyōintō "Etchu Jirōbee's Grave" (one unit) – Built in the 2nd year of {{ill|Ōan|en|Ōan|WD=Q1146245|lt=Ōan era|1=Ōan}} during the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanbokuchō period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}} ({{ill|1369|en|1369|WD=Q6211|lt=1369|1=1369}}); designated on March 1, Shōwa 50. Natural Monument: * The birthplace of {{ill|Himeharuzemi|ja|ヒメハルゼミ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himeharuzemi|1=Euterpnosia chibensis|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5976326}} (designated May 18, Heisei 2) == Access == * {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=Sanin Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|Kinosaki Onsen Station|en|Kinosaki Onsen Station|WD=Q1055689|lt=Kinosaki Onsen Station|1=Kinosaki Onsen Station}} – 15-minute walk. == See Also == * {{ill|Shisho Shrine|ja|四所神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shisho Shrine|1=Shisho Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11419883}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sisho-jinja}} * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6322096.html Shisho Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://www.kinosaki-spa.gr.jp/information/info/onsen.html Onsen-ji, Shisho Shrine, Gokuraku-ji] (Kinosaki Onsen Tourist Association) {{Shinto}} {{Kinosaki Onsen}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|四所神社 (豊岡市)|version=96678754|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:四所神社 (豊岡市)]] [[ja:四所神社]] {{Commons category|Sisho-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11419885}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Toyooka City]] [[Category:History of Toyooka]] [[Category:Onsen Shrines]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyooka, Hyōgo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
